dataset_title: Currency Composition of Official Foreign Exchange Reserves (COFER); SDMX dataflow IMF.STA:COFER, version 7.0.1; presentation title 'World - Official Foreign Exchange Reserves by Currency'
role_in_matrix: Supplies the currency-composition marginal of official reserve assets (USD/EUR/JPY/GBP/CNY/CHF/AUD/CAD/Other shares and USD nominal values) at the World aggregate level; pins the reserve-currency dimension of the FWTW matrix. It is a global/group-level marginal only, not a from-whom-to-whom cell: it has no creditor-by-debtor bilateral detail and no individual reporting-country detail (individual-country reserve composition is confidential).

structure.description: SDMX dataflow IMF.STA:COFER(7.0.1). Five dimensions plus time: COUNTRY, INDICATOR, FXR_CURRENCY, TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION, FREQUENCY; observation dimension TIME_PERIOD. COUNTRY in returned data is only the World/group aggregates G001=World, G110=Advanced Economies, G200=Emerging Market and Developing Economies (no individual-country series returned). INDICATOR codelist CL_COFER_INDICATOR(1.1.0): AFXRA=Allocated foreign exchange reserves, TFXRA=Total foreign exchange reserves, UFXRA=Unallocated foreign exchange reserves, TFXRA_IMP=Foreign exchange reserves that have been imputed (TFXRA_IMP returned zero series, i.e. not populated for the world group queried). TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION codelist CL_COFER_TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION(2.0.0): SHRO_PT=Shares, NV_USD=Nominal value (USD). FREQUENCY observed = Q (Quarterly).
structure.currencies_reported: FXR_CURRENCY codelist CL_COFER_FXR_CURRENCY(1.0.0), 14 codes (all prefixed 'Claims in'): CI_USD US dollar, CI_EUR Euro, CI_JPY Japanese yen, CI_GBP Pound sterling, CI_CNY Chinese yuan renminbi, CI_CHF Swiss franc, CI_AUD Australian dollar, CI_CAD Canadian dollar, CI_OTHC Other currencies, CI_T All currencies (total); plus legacy/historical currencies CI_DEM Deutsche mark, CI_FRF French franc, CI_NLG Dutch guilder, CI_ECU ECU. The eight actively reported reserve currencies plus 'Other currencies' and the 'All currencies' total are confirmed from the codelist.
structure.allocated_vs_unallocated: Distinguished as INDICATOR codes AFXRA (Allocated), UFXRA (Unallocated), and TFXRA (Total). VERIFIED FRICTION from live data: UFXRA (Unallocated) = 0 for 2025-Q1, 2025-Q2, 2025-Q3 and 2025-Q4, whereas it was non-zero historically (e.g. 96,537,422,288.6 USD in 1999-Q1). Correspondingly TFXRA equals AFXRA exactly in recent quarters (both 13,136,846,413,532.6 USD in 2025-Q4) but differed historically (1999-Q1: TFXRA 997,757,436,991.07 vs AFXRA 901,220,014,702.47). This live evidence is consistent with the IMF having eliminated the unallocated portion so that allocated composition now accounts for ~100% of world FX reserves, with revisions carried back, but the IMF's own prose statement of this methodology change could not be fetched (see unverified).

access.method: IMF SDMX 2.1 REST API at host api.imf.org (external endpoint). Dataflow metadata at /external/sdmx/2.1/dataflow/IMF.STA/COFER (HTTP 200). Data at /external/sdmx/2.1/data/IMF.STA,COFER/<COUNTRY>.<INDICATOR>.<FXR_CURRENCY>.<TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION>.<FREQUENCY> with query params such as lastNObservations and detail=serieskeysonly (HTTP 200). The human-facing data portal pages under data.imf.org (datasets/IMF.STA:COFER, the COFER dashboard, and the news brief) and the legacy www.imf.org COFER pages returned HTTP 403 to automated fetching in this environment, so they could not be read directly.
formats: SDMX-ML 2.1 (StructureSpecificData for data; Structure XML for DSD/codelists/dataflow)
access.api_or_bulk: Public SDMX 2.1 REST API confirmed working at https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/ . Bulk-download facility on the data.imf.org portal not verified (portal HTML 403); UNVERIFIED whether a CSV/bulk export is offered there.

frequency: Quarterly (FREQUENCY/FREQ code Q; CL_FREQ Q=Quarterly). Confirmed from the DSD and from quarterly TIME_PERIOD values (e.g. 2025-Q4) in live data.
reporting_lag: UNVERIFIED as a stated policy. Empirically, on 2026-06-28 the latest available observation was 2025-Q4 (e.g. USD allocated share 56.77% in 2025-Q4), implying roughly a one-quarter publication lag at the time checked, but the IMF's stated release-lag/schedule could not be read from a publisher page (data.imf.org and the news brief returned HTTP 403).
coverage: World total of official foreign exchange reserves plus the Advanced Economies (G110) and Emerging Market and Developing Economies (G200) groupings. Quarterly time series back to 1999 confirmed in live data (observations seen from 1999-Q1 through 2025-Q4). Individual reporting-country composition is not available in the public series (only world/group aggregates returned), consistent with COFER country-level confidentiality.
known_frictions: Unallocated reserves eliminated: UFXRA = 0 for all of 2025 (Q1-Q4) with revisions back through history; TFXRA now equals AFXRA in recent quarters. Allocated shares now effectively cover ~100% of world FX reserves. Any model splicing pre- and post-revision vintages, or relying on a non-zero unallocated residual, must account for this break. The IMF's own narrative of the change could not be fetched (403) and is recorded as unverified.

Currency composition is only available at World / group-aggregate level (G001/G110/G200); individual reporting-country composition is not in the public dataset. COFER country-level data are reported voluntarily and held confidential (confidentiality stated in secondary search results, NOT confirmed from a fetched IMF page).

Reserve shares are reported in USD-converted terms (TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION NV_USD nominal value and SHRO_PT shares); valuation/exchange-rate effects are embedded in level changes. The exact conversion convention (e.g. end-of-period market rates) is stated in secondary sources but NOT confirmed from a fetched IMF page.

Publisher human-facing pages (data.imf.org dataset/dashboard/news, www.imf.org/external/np/sta/cofer) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetching; grounding here relies on the machine-readable SDMX API at api.imf.org. The proxy reported no relay failures, indicating the 403s originate from IMF anti-bot controls rather than egress policy.

SDMX data-key path must carry exactly 5 dimensions in order COUNTRY.INDICATOR.FXR_CURRENCY.TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION.FREQUENCY; the COUNTRY filter appeared not to subset (queries returned all three group aggregates regardless). Consumers must filter G001 for World.
unverified: IMF's own prose statement and effective date of the elimination of the 'unallocated' portion (search results say starting 2025Q3 with revisions back to 2000Q1; the supporting IMF page data.imf.org/en/news/october 1 2025 cofer returned HTTP 403 and was not read). The 2025-all-quarters UFXRA=0 finding is from live API data and corroborates the change empirically.
Stated reporting/publication lag and release calendar (only empirically inferred ~1 quarter from latest vintage 2025-Q4 on 2026-06-28).
Number of COFER reporters and the voluntary/confidential reporting arrangement (secondary search results cite ~147 reporters; not confirmed from a fetched IMF page).
Exchange-rate conversion convention (end-of-period market rates) for USD-denominated values (secondary only).
Availability and format of a bulk/CSV download on the data.imf.org portal (portal HTML 403).
Whether TFXRA_IMP (imputed reserves) is populated for any country/group (returned zero series for the world-group query made).
notes: Grounded against the IMF machine-readable SDMX 2.1 API at api.imf.org on 2026-06-28: dataflow IMF.STA:COFER(7.0.1), codelists CL_COFER_FXR_CURRENCY(1.0.0), CL_COFER_INDICATOR(1.1.0), CL_COFER_TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION(2.0.0), and live observations. Tier A reflects that structure, currencies, allocated/unallocated distinction, frequency, country groups, history start (1999), and latest vintage (2025-Q4) were each read from official IMF endpoints, not memory. The human-facing data.imf.org and www.imf.org pages returned HTTP 403 to automated fetching (IMF anti-bot, not proxy egress per the proxy status showing no relay failures), so methodology prose, reporting lag, reporter count, and conversion convention remain UNVERIFIED and must not be filled from recall. For the matrix, treat COFER as a world-level currency-share marginal on official reserve assets, not as a bilateral FWTW source.
